title: lotononis involucrata subsp. peduncularis
intro: Lotononis involucrata subsp. peduncularis, also known as the "stick tree" or "bushwillow," is a plant species native to southern Africa, belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its leaves and stems are known for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Lotononis involucrata subsp. peduncularis include the potential treatment of various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a wound cleanser and skin toner. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
